mod_risk.title: Mod Security Scan
mod_risk.summary_3: ⚠️ Strongly not recommended! Found 3+ dangerous operations!
mod_risk.summary_1: ⚠️ Review carefully before loading! Found 1 dangerous operation.
mod_risk.dll_found: This mod contains external DLL files
mod_risk.disclaimer: The game developers assume no responsibility for any loss caused by running this mod.
mod_risk.dll_warn:
Cannot perform source-level malware detection — may be high-risk.
Only load if you trust the mod author or have verified the binary.
mod_risk.script_warn:
These files can execute arbitrary system commands — extreme security risk.
Only load if you trust the mod author.
mod_risk.gdscript_warn:
This mod uses GDScript which cannot be fully sandbox-isolated. Trust the author.
    Most benign mods don't use raw FileAccess.open(). Check the console log if unsure.
mod_risk.final_msg_fmt:
This mod has {0} high-risk operation(s).

The game developers and mod community assume no responsibility for this mod's behavior.
Contact the mod author or distributor for any damages.
Can't find the author? Tough luck.

Loading = you accept all risks and losses.
mod_risk.final_msg_safe:
This mod passed basic security scan — no obvious dangerous operations found.

⚠️ Static scanning cannot detect all risks. Malicious code may hide via string concatenation, encoding, dynamic loading, or reflection.

The game developers and mod community assume no responsibility.

Loading = you accept all risks.
mod_risk.final_accept: I accept all risks, load it
mod_risk.troll_msg: This mod has 3+ high-risk operations. Are you sure?
mod_risk.timed_btn: I accept all risks, continue loading ({0}s)
mod_risk.timed_ready: ✅ I accept all risks, continue loading

tutorial.welcome_desc: The main goal is to develop games, earn fans and money, and become the #1 company! If you're short on cash, try taking outsource contracts.

devmenu.kit_ok_msg: You can now develop console games

tut.step_waiting: Follow the guide — button unlocks automatically
tut.complete_desc:
Well done!

Good luck building your game empire! 🚀
tut.step1_desc:
Hello CEO! First, create your founder profile.

📝 Enter your name and company name
📊 Allocate 8 skill points (focus on one area)
🎭 Choose a founder trait (each gives unique bonuses)

Click « Start Game » when done!

(Drag the title bar to move this window)
tut.step2_desc:
Founder ready — now create your first game!

Click 📋 (Projects) at the bottom → New Project

Pick a Genre × Theme pair (try RPG × Fantasy),
set Budget and Scale (Medium is best to start).

Left panel shows estimated scores — higher is better!
tut.step3_desc:
After creating the project, you'll be taken to team assignment.

Select an idle team → Click « Assign Dev »

The project enters development phase!
tut.step4_desc:
Game quality isn't just about genre and theme — components matter too!

When creating a project, scroll to « Game Components »
Click the + button to equip a component.

Starter components: Pixel Aesthetics, Weather System, Open World, etc.
More components unlock via tech research.

Your starter engine already has 2D Rendering V1.
tut.step5_desc:
Boost your tech while developing!

Click 🔬 (Tech) at the bottom → pick an available tech
→ Click « Research » to assign a team.

Each completed tech improves your engine and all future games.
tut.step6_desc:
Game time advances monthly. Adjust speed anytime.

Number keys 1-8: change speed
Spacebar: pause/resume
WASD or right-click drag: rotate 3D camera
Mouse wheel: zoom in/out

Watch your funds — salaries & rent are due every month!
tut.step7_desc:
After development, the project enters the Polishing phase.

Polishing reduces bugs and raises score.
Aim for Bug count < 5 before release.

Click « Release » in project details to launch!

Released games earn monthly revenue and build fans.
tut.step8_desc:
Earn extra cash through outsourcing!

Click 📄 (Contracts) at the bottom → browse available tasks
Pick a suitable contract → Click « Accept »

Outsourcing provides stable side income
and keeps idle teams productive.
tut.step9_desc:
Use your revenue to grow the team!

Click 👥 (Team) at the bottom → Recruit

Each employee has unique skills and traits.
More staff = run multiple projects at once.

Office capacity limits headcount — upgrade when full!
tut.step10_desc:
Too many employees? Time to move!

Click 🏢 (Company) → Finance → Move to bigger office
Larger offices hold more people and reduce fatigue.

Click 🏢 (Company) → Engine for engine management.
Set business model: Closed/Open Source/Buyout/Subscription/Royalty
Higher reputation = more licensing revenue.
tut.step11_desc:
Employees are your most valuable asset.

📚 Training: improve skills (costs ¥30,000)
💭 Dreams: each employee has personal dreams — fulfilling them boosts loyalty
🧠 Mental state: high fatigue or low satisfaction may cause resignation

Check team status regularly and keep morale high!
tut.step12_desc:
The game has even more systems:

🖥 Server Room — infrastructure for online games
⚔ Corporate Warfare — compete with AI studios
🏢 Company Overview — market data & rivals
📖 Encyclopedia (F1) — game mechanics guide
🏆 Achievement Hall (F2) — unlock achievements

Click corresponding icons at the bottom!
(Tip: use the ❓ button to revisit tutorial steps)
tut.step13_desc:
Don't forget to save your progress!

Press ESC to open the pause menu
💾 Save — manual save
📂 Load — load a save

Auto-save occurs every quarter.
Adjust auto-save interval in Settings.
tut.step14_desc:
You've mastered all the essentials of running a game studio!

✅ Founder creation
✅ Project creation, components & development
✅ Tech research & engine iteration
✅ Polish, release & post-launch operations
✅ Hiring, expansion & office upgrades
✅ Employee training, outsourcing & contracts
✅ Save & load

Now go build your game empire! 🚀

crisis.server_crash_desc: Launch day traffic overwhelmed your servers! Players are getting error 503. Act fast!
crisis.server_emergency_result: Servers stabilized, but the emergency fix left technical debt
crisis.server_apology_result: Players accepted the apology, but reputation took a hit
crisis.feature_creep_desc: The design team proposed an exciting new feature — but it will delay development.
crisis.creep_accept_result: Gameplay depth increased, but progress delayed and tech debt rises
crisis.creep_accept2_result: Game potential skyrockets, but development cycle extends significantly
crisis.creep_reject_result: Team is a bit disappointed, but project stays on schedule
crisis.creep_cut: Cut old features to make room
crisis.creep_cut_result: New features replace old, but completed work is wasted
crisis.creep_reset_result: Project back on track, but some potential lost
crisis.creep_ship_result: Game finally releases, but with many bugs and poor quality
crisis.creep_again_desc: The publisher demands a major feature — it will fundamentally change the game
crisis.creep_hell_desc: The project is severely overdue, the team is exhausted — a decision must be made
crisis.debt_warn_desc: Code quality is declining. If this continues, major trouble awaits
crisis.debt_collapse_desc: Code is completely out of control! Every change introduces new bugs
crisis.debt_meltdown_desc: The worst has happened! The code is completely unmaintainable
crisis.debt_ignore_result: Short-term progress is faster, but bugs are skyrocketing
crisis.debt_crunch_result: Barely holding on, but the team is nearing collapse
crisis.debt_refactor_result: Code quality improves, but progress temporarily slows
crisis.debt_rewrite_result: Painful but necessary. Code is finally maintainable
crisis.debt_abandon_result: Starting from scratch, but design docs are preserved
crisis.publisher_desc: The publisher wants to add microtransactions, promising extra budget
crisis.publisher_accept_result: Extra budget obtained, but player trust decreases
crisis.publisher_reject_result: Lost extra budget, but gained player goodwill
crisis.publisher_double_down_result: Short-term revenue explodes, but community turns hostile
crisis.publisher_revert_result: Players cheer, but lost microtransaction revenue and dev investment
crisis.publisher_backlash_desc: The player community is strongly opposing the microtransactions. Reputation is deteriorating
crisis.server_bandaid_result: Works temporarily, but tech debt accumulates
crisis.server_refactor_result: Architecture rewritten. Long-term stability, but short-term delay
crisis.server_overhaul_result: Code rewrite complete. Stability and dev efficiency greatly improved
crisis.server_ignore_result: Servers become increasingly unstable. Players leaving in droves
crisis.server_chaos_desc: Emergency scaling has left the server architecture in chaos. New feature development is crippled
crisis.server_debt_desc: Accumulated tech debt has finally exploded! Servers may crash again at any moment
